title: How to Build Your Own Table When There's No Seat for You in Leadership
guestBio: Neetu Sadhwani is a visionary entrepreneur and leader who exemplifies building something meaningful from the ground up. Known for her courage in leaving comfort behind to chase purpose, she's a powerful voice on creating your own opportunities rather than waiting for permission.
excerpt: In the premiere episode of The Manage Her, Aimee sits down with powerhouse Neetu Sadhwani to discuss the bold decision to leave comfort behind and build your own opportunities. This conversation dives deep into burnout, courage, and why waiting for a seat at the table is no longer an option.
metaDescription: Neetu Sadhwani shares how she built her own table in leadership, overcame burnout, and found the courage to chase purpose over comfort on The Manage Her podcast.
keyTakeaways: Building your own table is more powerful than waiting for someone else to offer you a seat.
Leaving comfort behind for purpose requires courage, but it's essential for authentic leadership.
Burnout is often a signal that you're not aligned with your true purpose and values.
Grace, grit, and vision are the three pillars needed to build something meaningful from scratch.
Believing in yourself even when it's hard is a non-negotiable skill for women leaders.
Real balance isn't about perfect equilibrium but about making intentional choices aligned with your priorities.
Women are more capable than they think, but permission-giving starts from within.

pullQuotes.text: Why waiting for a seat at the table just isn't it anymore — she built her own

content:
Welcome to the very first episode of The Manage Her podcast, where we're setting the tone for everything this show represents: honest conversations, powerful women, and the kind of permission-giving energy that reminds you just how capable you really are.

I'm your host Aimee Rickabus, and I couldn't be more excited to kick things off with the incredible Neetu Sadhwani. If you've ever felt like you're waiting for someone else to make room for you at the leadership table, this conversation is going to shift something fundamental in how you think about building your career and your life.

The Courage to Leave Comfort Behind

One of the most powerful themes that emerged from my conversation with Neetu is this idea of having the courage to leave comfort behind in pursuit of something bigger. We live in a world that constantly tells us to play it safe, to take the predictable path, to wait for permission. But what Neetu discovered, and what she shares so beautifully in this episode, is that real fulfillment comes when you're willing to step into the unknown.

The truth is, comfort can become a cage. You might have a good job, a steady paycheck, and the approval of everyone around you, but if you're not aligned with your purpose, you're slowly dying inside. Neetu talks about that moment when she realized she had to make a choice: stay safe or step into her power. The decision wasn't easy, but it was necessary.

This resonates so deeply with me because I see it everywhere in my work with women leaders. You have this incredible vision, this knowing that you're meant for something more, but the gap between where you are and where you want to be feels insurmountable. What Neetu teaches us is that the bridge across that gap is built one courageous decision at a time.

Building Your Own Table

Here's where the conversation gets really juicy. We've all heard the phrase about getting a seat at the table, right? Well, Neetu flips that entire narrative on its head. Instead of waiting for someone else to make room for her, she decided to build her own table. And not just any table, but one that reflects her values, her vision, and her definition of success.

This mindset shift is everything. When you're waiting for a seat at someone else's table, you're operating from scarcity. You're hoping someone will see your value and invite you in. But when you build your own table, you're operating from abundance. You're creating the rules, setting the standards, and inviting others to join you in something meaningful.

The beauty of building your own table is that you get to decide what it looks like. You don't have to conform to outdated leadership models that were never designed with women in mind. You can create something that honors your strengths, accommodates your life, and makes space for the kind of leader you actually want to be.

The Real Talk on Burnout

We couldn't have this conversation without diving deep into burnout, because let's be honest, it's an epidemic among ambitious women. Neetu shares her own experience with burnout and the insights that came from it. What she discovered is that burnout isn't just about being too busy or working too hard. It's often a signal that you're not aligned with your purpose.

When you're pouring your energy into something that doesn't light you up, when you're trying to succeed in systems that weren't built for you, when you're constantly pushing against your natural rhythms and instincts, your body and mind will eventually say no. Burnout becomes the wake-up call that forces you to examine whether you're living your life or someone else's version of what your life should be.

The recovery from burnout isn't just about rest and self-care, though those things matter. It's about realigning with what actually matters to you. It's about having the courage to make different choices, even when those choices disappoint other people or challenge their expectations of you.

Grace, Grit, and Vision

Throughout our conversation, Neetu embodies what I call the trifecta of successful leadership: grace, grit, and vision. These aren't just nice qualities to have; they're essential tools for building something meaningful from the ground up.

Grace is about how you show up in the world. It's about maintaining your integrity and treating people well, even when things get challenging. It's about leading with empathy and understanding that everyone is fighting their own battles.

Grit is about your willingness to keep going when things get hard. And they will get hard. Building anything worthwhile requires perseverance, resilience, and the ability to pivot when your original plan isn't working.

Vision is about seeing possibilities that others can't see yet. It's about holding onto a clear picture of what you're creating, even when everyone around you thinks you're crazy. It's about trusting your instincts and betting on yourself when no one else will.

Believing in Yourself When It's Hard

Perhaps the most important theme from this conversation is the idea of believing in yourself even when it's hard. Especially when it's hard. Because here's what I know to be true: if you don't believe in yourself, no one else will. And believing in yourself isn't a one-time decision. It's a daily practice, especially when you're building something new.

There will be days when you question everything. When the criticism feels too loud, when the progress feels too slow, when the mountain you're climbing seems impossibly tall. In those moments, you have to come back to what you know to be true about yourself and your vision.

This is where community becomes so important. Surrounding yourself with other women who understand the journey, who can reflect back your strength when you can't see it yourself, who can remind you of how far you've already come. That's part of what The Manage Her is about, creating that community of women who are committed to lifting each other up.

As we wrap up this inaugural episode, I want you to remember that you are more capable than you think. The dreams you have, the vision that keeps you up at night, the feeling that you're meant for something more? Trust that. Honor that. And most importantly, take action on that.

This is just the beginning of our journey together on The Manage Her podcast. Every episode, we're going to dive into these real, honest conversations about what it means to be a woman building something meaningful in the world. And I can't wait to continue this conversation with you.

quiz.title: Are You Ready to Build Your Own Table?
quiz.description: Discover whether you're someone who waits for permission or creates your own opportunities. Take this quiz to uncover your unique leadership approach and how you can step into your power.

questions.question: When you imagine your ideal work situation five years from now, what feels most exciting to you?

options.text: Leading something I created from scratch, even if it means starting over
Having a senior role where I can implement my strategic vision
Being recognized as an expert in my field with increasing responsibility
Working with amazing people on meaningful projects that make a difference

questions.question: You're in a meeting where you have a brilliant idea, but it challenges the current approach. What's your typical response?

options.text: I speak up immediately—good ideas shouldn't wait for the 'right' moment
I think it through first, then schedule time to present it properly with supporting data
I share it if asked directly, but I'm careful about how I frame it
I find a way to suggest it that builds on what others have already said

questions.question: When you think about taking a big career risk, what's usually your biggest concern?

options.text: That I might not execute my vision as well as I imagine it
That I haven't considered all the potential obstacles and backup plans
That I might be giving up security and progress I've already made
That it might negatively impact the people who depend on me

questions.question: How do you typically respond when you're feeling burned out or misaligned at work?

options.text: I start planning my exit strategy and researching new possibilities
I analyze what's not working and develop a plan to address it systematically
I focus on the positive aspects and try to find ways to grow within my current situation
I talk it through with trusted friends and family to get their perspective

questions.question: What does 'believing in yourself' look like for you on a practical level?

options.text: Trusting my instincts even when others think I'm being too bold
Having confidence in my ability to solve problems I haven't encountered yet
Knowing that my consistent effort and dedication will pay off over time
Remembering that my perspective and contributions matter, even when I doubt myself

questions.question: When you hear the phrase 'building your own table,' what's your honest first reaction?

options.text: Yes! I've been waiting for permission to do exactly that
It sounds appealing, but I want to understand exactly what that would look like first
It's intriguing, but I'm not sure I'm ready to leave behind what I've already built
I love the idea, but I wonder if there's a way to do it that brings others along too

results.title: The Table Builder
results.description: You're naturally drawn to creating new opportunities rather than waiting for someone else to invite you in. You have strong instincts about when it's time to leave comfort behind and the courage to act on them. Your challenge is trusting that your vision is worth the risk. This episode will validate your table-building instincts and give you the framework of grace, grit, and vision you need to build something truly meaningful.

results.title: The Thoughtful Strategist
results.description: You believe in thorough preparation and careful planning before making big moves. You're incredibly capable but sometimes get stuck in analysis mode when what you really need is to trust yourself and leap. You have all the skills to build your own table—you just need permission to believe in your readiness. This episode will help you recognize that waiting for the 'perfect' moment might be holding you back from your true purpose.

results.title: The Steady Climber
results.description: You're committed to growth and achievement, but you prefer working within existing structures rather than creating entirely new ones. You're incredibly reliable and others trust you to deliver, but you might be playing it too safe. Your superpower is your consistency, but your growth edge is learning when comfort becomes a cage. This episode will inspire you to consider that building your own table might be the next level of leadership you've been preparing for all along.

results.title: The Harmony Seeker
results.description: You're deeply thoughtful about how your choices affect others and you value collaboration and balance above all else. You might struggle with the idea of 'building your own table' because it feels selfish or disruptive. But your natural ability to consider multiple perspectives is actually a leadership superpower. This episode will help you understand that building your own table can be an act of service—creating space for others who also need a seat.
